title: Metric Dictionary — Core Page and Data Model
story: As a data analyst, I want a Metric Dictionary that maps business metrics to their definitions, owners, and related fields, so that I can maintain a centralized semantic layer connecting metrics to the underlying data model.
description:
Introduces the Metric Dictionary as a new top-level feature in DataTools Pro. The dictionary provides a centralized registry of business metrics with taxonomy categorization, ownership, and linkage to Salesforce objects and fields.

## Metric Record Structure

Each metric is a top-level record with the following columns:

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Metric Name | Primary name of the metric |
| Line of Business | Business unit taxonomy (many-to-one, designate a primary) |
| Topic | Subject area taxonomy (many-to-one, designate a primary) |
| Definition | Business definition of the metric |
| Primary Field | Linked Salesforce object + field |
| Aliases | Alternative names (e.g., "CPA", "Cost per Funded" for "Cost Per Acquisition") |
| Owner | Metric owner |
| Dimensionality | Analytical dimensions |
| Reports | Related reports |
| Notes | Freeform notes |

## Aliases

Aliases are stored in a separate `scorecard_aliases` table with the schema:

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Id | Primary key |
| MetricId | Foreign key to the metric record |
| Alias Name | The alternative name |
| Is Primary | Boolean flag for the primary name |

Aliases are displayed as semicolon-separated values in the data table (e.g., "CPA; Cost per Funded").

## Navigation

The Metrics tab is added alongside Dictionary and Migration tabs. Page titles update dynamically based on the active tab:
- Dictionary → "Field Dictionary"
- Migration → "Migration Scorecard"
- Metrics → "Metrics"

## Taxonomy Tables

Line of Business, Topic, and Dimensionality are taxonomy values stored in a metadata table and associated with user accounts. Picklist values are sourced from the `salesforce_object_field_picklist_value` table.

## Data Model Considerations

Development was paused after the initial implementation to accommodate data model refinements documented in the Confluence article "Metrics Dictionary". The many-to-one relationships for LOB, Topic, Owner, and Definition each need a designated primary value.
acceptance_criteria: A new Metrics tab appears alongside Dictionary and Migration tabs
Page title updates dynamically when switching between tabs (Dictionary → 'Field Dictionary', Migration → 'Migration Scorecard', Metrics → 'Metrics')
Metrics data table displays all specified columns: Metric Name, Line of Business, Topic, Definition, Primary Field, Aliases, Owner, Dimensionality, Reports, Notes
User can create, edit, and delete metric records inline via the DataTable editor
Aliases are stored in a separate scorecard_aliases table with MetricId, Alias Name, and Is Primary fields
Aliases display as semicolon-separated values in the table column
Line of Business, Topic, and Dimensionality render as picklist values sourced from the salesforce_object_field_picklist_value table
Each taxonomy category (LOB, Topic, Owner, Definition) supports a designated primary value
ux_changes: New Metrics tab added to the main navigation alongside Dictionary and Migration. The page renders a full DataTable with inline editing for all metric columns. Aliases display as semicolon-separated text. Page title updates dynamically based on the active tab.
middleware_changes: Controller updated to handle CRUD operations for metrics, including reading/writing aliases from the separate scorecard_aliases table and rendering picklist values from the salesforce_object_field_picklist_value table. jQuery DataTable editor configured to manage data across multiple database tables.
data_model_changes: New metric records table with columns for all metric attributes. New scorecard_aliases table (Id, MetricId, Alias Name, Is Primary) for storing multiple alternative names per metric. Taxonomy values (LOB, Topic, Dimensionality) stored in metadata tables associated with user accounts. Picklist values sourced from salesforce_object_field_picklist_value table.
api_changes: Endpoints for metric CRUD operations including create, read, update, and delete. Alias management endpoints that read/write to the scorecard_aliases table. Picklist value retrieval endpoint updated to source from salesforce_object_field_picklist_value table.
implementation_notes: Built on jQuery DataTables with Editor plugin. The primary technical challenge was managing data across two database tables (scorecard for metrics, scorecard_aliases for aliases) within a single DataTable. The controller was refactored to source picklist values from the salesforce_object_field_picklist_value table instead of hardcoded values. Development was paused after initial implementation to refine the data model per Confluence documentation, particularly around many-to-one relationships for LOB, Topic, Owner, and Definition fields.
comment_summary: Developer and product owner iterated on the metric record structure, clarifying that metrics are top-level records (not nested under objects). Aliases were defined as alternative names stored in a separate database table and displayed as semicolon-separated text. The main technical challenge was configuring DataTables to manage data across multiple database tables. Development was paused after initial implementation to accommodate data model refinements documented in Confluence.
